core_model.structure_summary: A linear second-order hyperbolic equation for a scalar field on a one-dimensional continuum, with second derivatives in both space and time, a first-order-in-time dissipative term, and a zeroth-order term, all with time-dependent coefficients arising from a time-varying domain, which upon mode decomposition reduces to second-order oscillator equations with time-dependent damping and frequency that can yield exponentially growing amplitudes.
core_model.canonical_form: \partial_x^2 u = a(t)\,\partial_t^2 u + b(t)\,\partial_t u + c(t)\,u; \qquad u(x,t) = \sum_n A_n(t)\,\varphi_n(x), \quad \ddot{A}_n + b(t)\,\dot{A}_n + \Omega_n^2(t)\,A_n = 0

variables.meaning: scalar field over the one-dimensional domain (a second dual field obeys the same equation)
time-dependent amplitude of each spatial eigenmode after separation of variables
coordinate along the one-dimensional continuum with a moving endpoint
time-dependent coefficients (per-unit-length densities and loss rates) whose time variation encodes the moving boundary and drives parametric amplification

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A second-order linear oscillator with time-dependent damping coefficient and time-dependent natural frequency, whose parametric time variation can convert oscillation into exponential amplitude growth.

notes: Two candidate framings: the full damped hyperbolic equation with time-dependent coefficients (chosen as core) and the per-mode parametric damped oscillator ODE it separates into (chosen as skeleton, since the paper's conclusions about oscillation character and exponential growth are read off the mode equations via a WKB-type two-exponential ansatz). The model is already deterministic; the skeleton is the mode-reduced form.
